user_prompt:
User Requirement: Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.

Reference Directory Structure (similar case): <dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [T, U, alphat, epsilon, k, nut, p, p_rgh]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [g, momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>

There are 8 files in Directory: 0,
There are 4 files in Directory: system,
There are 3 files in Directory: constant

Make sure you generate all the necessary files for the user's requirements.Do not include any gmsh files like .geo etc. in the subtasks.Only include blockMesh or snappyHexMesh if the user hasnt requested for gmsh mesh or user isnt using an external uploaded custom meshPlease generate the output as structured JSON.
response: subtasks=[SubtaskModel(file_name='T', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='U', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='alphat', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='epsilon', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='k', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='nut', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='p', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='p_rgh', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='g', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant')]
